title: Goldman Sachs Sees Petco (WOOF) Poised for 2026 Growth Driven by Unique Strength in High-Margin Veterinary Services
text: Goldman Sachs Sees Petco (WOOF) Poised for 2026 Growth. Analyst Perspective: Goldman Sachs noted Petco's unique positioning in the pet retail sector with integrated veterinary services capabilities. Competitive Advantage: The company's 300 veterinary hospitals and growing grooming network differentiate it from pure-play pet merchandise retailers. High-Margin Services: Veterinary services command premium pricing and deliver significantly higher margins than commodity pet merchandise. Goldman Sachs View: The analyst highlighted that Petco's services business has potential to drive earnings growth disproportionate to revenue growth. 2026 Catalysts: 1) Expansion of veterinary hospital footprint beyond current 300 locations. 2) Continued margin expansion as services mix increases. 3) Fresh food expansion tapping into high-frequency, high-spend customer segment. 4) Merchandising innovation driving comparable sales recovery. Valuation: At Goldman Sachs' price target of $4.50, the stock offers compelling risk/reward for investors believing in the services growth narrative. Risks: Competitive entry from other retailers, consumer spending weakness, execution risks on expansion plans.
